title: Patalathri Narasimhar Temple
tagline: Patalathri Narasimhar Temple — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

overview.short: The Padalathri Narasimhar Temple or Narasimhar Temple (also called Singaperumal Koil) is dedicated to Hindu god Vishnu (Narasimha) located in Singaperumal Koil, near Chennai City,
overview.description: The Padalathri Narasimhar Temple or Narasimhar Temple (also called Singaperumal Koil) is dedicated to Hindu god Vishnu (Narasimha) located in Singaperumal Koil, near Chennai City, in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Constructed as rock-cut architecture, it is dedicated to Vishnu, who is worshipped as Narasimha, and his consort Lakshmi as Ahobilavalli. The temple was built by the Pallavas during the 8th century. Earlier in Kanchipuram district, it is now under Chengalpattu district.

topPlaces.name: Singaperumalkoil
topPlaces.description: Singaperumal Koil is a satellite town city of Chennai located between Chengalpattu and Maraimalai Nagar in Chengalpattu district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. It is a part of the area Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority (CMDA) in the Chennai Metropolitan Area.

topPlaces.name: Kachabeswarar temple, Thirukachur
topPlaces.description: Kachabeswarar Temple, Thirukachur is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva, located in Thirukachur, a village in Kanchipuram district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu. Shiva is worshipped as Kachabeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.

topPlaces.name: Marundeeswarar Temple, Thirukachur
topPlaces.description: Marundeeswarar Temple or Oushadheeshwar Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to the deity Shiva. Shiva is worshipped as Marundeeswarar, and is represented by the lingam.

topPlaces.name: Maraimalai Nagar
topPlaces.description: Maraimalai Nagar is a municipality town in South Chennai at Chengalpattu district, Tamil Nadu, India. Lying in the southern part of the Chennai metropolitan area, it is a satellite town of Chennai, the state capital, around 50 km from Downtown Chennai (Egmore) and about 15 km from Vandalur.

topPlaces.name: Mahindra World City, Chennai
topPlaces.description: Mahindra World City, New Chennai, Chengalpattu district, Tamil Nadu, is India's first integrated business city. It is a public–private partnership promoted by the Mahindra Group and TIDCO. It is situated near Maraimalai Nagar a southern suburb of Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Paranur
topPlaces.description: Paranur is a village in India near the busy GST Road or National Highway 45. It has a railway station and few hundred houses, and is 44 km from Chennai International Airport in Tirusulam.

topPlaces.name: Appur
topPlaces.description: Appur is a panchayat village located in Chengalpattu district of Tamil Nadu, India. It is located about 56 kilometers from Chennai.

topPlaces.name: Kattankulathur block
topPlaces.description: Kattankulathur block is a revenue block in the Chengalpattu district of Tamil Nadu, India. It has a total of 39 panchayat villages.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Patalathri Narasimhar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Patalathri Narasimhar Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Patalathri Narasimhar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chennai International Airport (32 km) and the nearest railway station is Chennai Central (46 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Patalathri Narasimhar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Patalathri Narasimhar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Patalathri Narasimhar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Patalathri Narasimhar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Patalathri Narasimhar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Singaperumalkoil, Kachabeswarar temple, Thirukachur, Marundeeswarar Temple, Thirukachur, Maraimalai Nagar, Mahindra World City, Chennai.
